channel.title: 2N Telekomunikace a.s. — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by 2N Telekomunikace a.s. (2n).

item.title: CVE-2025-59787 · 5.3 MEDIUM — HTTP 5XX Internal Server Errors
item.description: 2N Access Commander application version 3.4.2 and prior returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error responses when receiving malformed or manipulated requests, indicating improper handling of invalid input and potential security or availability impacts.

item.title: CVE-2025-59786 · 6 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Cookies are not Invalidated upon Logout and Password Change
item.description: 2N Access Commander version 3.4.2 and prior improperly invalidates session tokens, allowing multiple session cookies to remain active after logout in web application.

item.title: CVE-2025-59785 · 5.3 MEDIUM — API - Insufficient Input Validation
item.description: Improper validation of API end-point in 2N Access Commander version 3.4.2 and prior allows attacker to bypass password policy for backup file encryption. This vulnerability can only be exploited after authenticating with administrator privileges.

item.title: CVE-2025-59784 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Log Pollution - Control Characters Not Escaped
item.description: 2N Access Commander version 3.4.1 and prior is vulnerable to log pollution. Certain parameters sent over API may be included in the logs without prior validation or sanitisation. This vulnerability can only be exploited after authenticating with admi…

item.title: CVE-2025-59783 · 8.8 HIGH — OS Command Injection over API
item.description: API endpoint for user synchronization in 2N Access Commander version 3.4.1 did not have a sufficient input validation allowing for OS command injection. This vulnerability can only be exploited after authenticating with administrator privileges.
